# logging

```go
import "github.com/neochaotic/powerlab/backend/pkg/logging"
```

Package logging is PowerLab's structured logger, built on log/slog.

Every backend service constructs a Logger from a Config populated by environment variables, then logs through the Logger interface. Correlation IDs from pkg/tracing are auto\-injected when a request context is passed in.

Example:

```
cfg := logging.Config{Level: "info", Format: "json"}
logger, err := logging.New(cfg)
if err != nil {
    return err
}
logger.Info(ctx, "service started", slog.String("addr", addr))
```

See ADR\-0012 for the rationale behind slog over zap.

## Index

- [type Config](<#Config>)

- [type Logger](<#Logger>)

<a name="Config"></a>
## type Config

Config controls how a Logger is constructed.

Zero values are sensible: Level defaults to "info", Format to "console", Writer to os.Stdout.

```go
type Config struct {
    // Level is one of "debug", "info", "warn", "error" (case-insensitive).
    // Empty string defaults to "info".
    Level string

    // Format is "console" (human-readable) or "json" (machine-readable),
    // case-insensitive. Empty string defaults to "console".
    Format string

    // Writer is where log lines are emitted. Defaults to os.Stdout.
    Writer io.Writer
}
```

<a name="CorrelationIDKey"></a>
## type CorrelationIDKey

CorrelationIDKey is the context.Context key under which a correlation ID may be stored. The Logger's Handler reads from this key on every emission and injects the value as a "correlation\_id" attribute when non\-empty.

pkg/tracing will use this same key when it lands; defining it here keeps pkg/logging free of dependencies for now and lets services use correlation IDs even before tracing is wired up.

```go
type CorrelationIDKey struct{}
```

<a name="Logger"></a>
## type Logger

Logger is the structured logging contract every PowerLab service uses.

Methods take a context.Context first so that the underlying handler can extract the correlation ID \(and any future request\-scoped attributes\) without callers having to remember to pass it explicitly.

There is intentionally no Fatal method. Process termination is the responsibility of pkg/lifecycle, which logs through Error and then triggers a graceful shutdown.

```go
type Logger interface {
    Debug(ctx context.Context, msg string, attrs ...slog.Attr)
    Info(ctx context.Context, msg string, attrs ...slog.Attr)
    Warn(ctx context.Context, msg string, attrs ...slog.Attr)
    Error(ctx context.Context, msg string, err error, attrs ...slog.Attr)

    // With returns a new Logger that adds the given attributes to every
    // subsequent log line. Useful for service-scoped or component-scoped
    // loggers (e.g. logger.With(slog.String("component", "gateway"))).
    With(attrs ...slog.Attr) Logger
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func New

```go
func New(cfg Config) (Logger, error)
```

New constructs a Logger from the given Config.

Returns an error if Level or Format is set to an unrecognized value. Empty strings are treated as defaults.
